content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer NNN.

Find a permutation†^\dagger† PPP of the integers 111 to NNN such that

Pi≥(Pi+1−Pi−1)2P_i \geq \left( P_{i+1} - P_{i-1} \right)^2Pi​≥(Pi+1​−Pi−1​)2holds for every index iii from 222 to N−1N-1N−1.

It can be proved that at least one solution exists.

If multiple solutions exist, print any of them.

†^\dagger† A permutation of the integers 111 to NNN is an array of length NNN that contains every integer from 111 to NNN.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line NNN space-separated integers — the permutation PPP for which the above condition holds.


If multiple solutions exist, you may print any of them.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 10510^5105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: There is no index iii between 222 and N−1N-1N−1, so the condition is automatically satisfied for any permutation of length 222.
Test case 333: It can be observed that:

P2=4≥(1−3)2=4P_2 = 4 \geq (1 - 3)^2 = 4P2​=4≥(1−3)2=4
P3=3≥(4−5)2=1P_3 = 3 \geq (4 - 5)^2 = 1P3​=3≥(4−5)2=1
P4=5≥(3−2)2=1P_4 = 5 \geq (3 - 2)^2 = 1P4​=5≥(3−2)2=1
